body: Is this multi-arch? The image we are building with depot runners is multi-arch.

body:
No, this is a single arch image build.

Also, I can't find us building multi-arch images on Depot. Would you mind pointing me to where we're doing that?

body:
I am sorry for the confusion. Multi-arch builds are for our github.com/coder/images repo. Depot does that automatically. 

Dogfood is still `amd64` only.

        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'

body:
We always build the images and only push them on the main branch.
This aligns with the 'Build and push Non-Nix image' step, which also builds the image without pushing it and with the behavior before the refactoring.

Or is it only useful for building the Docker image?
It's only useful as a trigger for the dogfood environment to pull a new image.
How are we keeping this file up to date? Is it manual, i.e., whenever someone updates the Nix flake?

body:
Are you asking how we keep our flake up to date?

When the go.mod contents change, we have a script at `scripts/update-flake.sh` that updates the vendor hash for the Go module, the proto gen, and the dogfood nix.hash file. As is the current status quo, this will have to be performed manually on each go.mod change.

The nix flake's lock file can be updated using `nix flake update` (assuming the nix commands and flake experiment are enabled on one's machine).

body: `./scripts/update_flake.sh` seems to need this variable defined.

Is this only expected to work on Linux systems?
```
error: flake 'git+file:///Users/cian/src/coder/coder' does not provide attribute 'packages.aarch64-darwin.dev_image', 'legacyPackages.aarch64-darwin.dev_image' or 'dev_image'

```
Yes, building an OCI image is only supported on Linux systems.

Personally/locally, I've spun up a VM with nixOS and configured it as a remote builder, but any other Linux OS will do.

It's the same thing that Docker Desktop/Colima/OrbStack/... do for one under the hood but without the hood and magic abstractions part.
